title: Cooling Tahini & Tomato Noodle Soup
summary: Cold, creamy sesame and soy noodles with juicy tomatoes and a punchy crispy chilli dressing. It’s silky, savoury and seriously refreshing on a boiling hot day.

method.description: Add the water (you can substitute half of the water with ice cubes if you’ve got a good blender), tofu, miso, tahini, sesame oil and rice vinegar into a blender. Blend until smooth, season to taste with salt (this dish is served ice cold, so that the flavours will be slightly muted from the temperature). I recommend heavily seasoning to your taste.) Place in the fridge or freezer until cold.

Meanwhile, trim and thinly slice the spring onions, and chop the tomatoes into 2-3cm bite-sized chunks. Add both into a bowl with the crispy chilli oil (getting as much of the chilli crisp sendiment as possible) and vinegar, stir together, taste and season with salt, if needed.
Bring a pot of water to the boil. Cook the soba noodles following pack instructions, then drain and rinse under cold running water until completely cool.
Divide the noodles between bowls. Pour over the chilled sesame broth and finish with a generous spoonful of the tomato salad on top.

tips: If you can’t find silken tofu, omit and substitute the water with unsweetened 100% soy milk instead.
Soba Noodles → Vermicelli or ramen noodles
Chilli Crisp → Sriracha or a splash of chilli oil

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
